snippets.title: Defining fixtures (with teardown via yield)
snippets.explanation: `sample_chunks` is a plain return-value fixture. `vector_store` uses `yield` so the post-test cleanup is right next to the setup.

snippets.title: Sharing fixtures across files (`conftest.py`)
snippets.explanation: Define once in `conftest.py`; every test in the directory tree can name the fixture without importing.

snippets.title: Parametrized fixtures
snippets.explanation: Test runs once per param — 4 cases here. Useful when many tests need to vary the same dimension.

snippets.title: Mocking an LLM client with patch
snippets.code:
from unittest.mock import patch, MagicMock
from ragapp.summarizer import summarize


@patch("ragapp.summarizer.openai_client")
def test_summarize_calls_llm(mock_client):
    fake_response = MagicMock()
    fake_response.choices[0].message.content = "summary text"
    mock_client.chat.completions.create.return_value = fake_response

    result = summarize("long document")

    assert result == "summary text"
    mock_client.chat.completions.create.assert_called_once()
    call_args = mock_client.chat.completions.create.call_args
    assert "long document" in call_args.kwargs["messages"][0]["content"]
snippets.explanation: Patch where `summarize` *uses* `openai_client`. Assert the result and that the LLM was called once with the document in the message.

snippets.title: Faking time with monkeypatch
snippets.code:
def test_backoff_sleeps_expected_durations(monkeypatch):
    sleeps: list[float] = []
    monkeypatch.setattr("ragapp.retry.time.sleep", lambda s: sleeps.append(s))

    call_with_retry(flaky_429_fn, max_retries=3)

    assert sleeps == [1.0, 2.0, 4.0]   # exponential backoff
snippets.explanation: `monkeypatch.setattr` replaces `time.sleep` *only for this test*. The test verifies the backoff *sequence* without ever waiting.

snippets.title: Built-in capsys and caplog
snippets.code:
import logging


def test_prints_warning_on_429(capsys, caplog):
    with caplog.at_level(logging.WARNING):
        handle_429()
    assert any("429" in r.message for r in caplog.records)

    captured = capsys.readouterr()
    assert "Rate-limited" in captured.out
snippets.explanation: `caplog` captures log records (structured); `capsys` captures stdout/stderr (text). Use them for observability assertions.
